message:
test(raid5): silence unused Cx binding in btrfs_multi_device_raid5_read_conforms

The let cx = Cx::for_testing() binding in btrfs_multi_device_raid5_read_conforms
is no longer referenced — the read path under test was refactored to thread the
context implicitly via the device set, so the local cx is dead. With clippy's
unused_variables lint promoted to a hard error in this workspace
(`-D warnings` per AGENTS.md), the dead binding broke `cargo check --all-targets`.

Rename to _cx rather than dropping the call entirely: Cx::for_testing() still
sets up thread-local test fixtures (per crates/ffs-core), and removing the
constructor would silently change those fixtures' lifetime. The leading
underscore preserves construction while signaling intentional non-use.

No behavior change.
